faqs.question: What types of solutions does STMicroelectronics offer?
faqs.answer: STMicroelectronics offers comprehensive solutions across multiple domains: Motor Control solutions utilizing STM32 microcontrollers and power discretes for efficient motor drives; Power Conversion solutions leveraging SiC MOSFETs and GaN devices for high-efficiency energy conversion; IoT and Connectivity solutions combining sensors, microcontrollers, and wireless connectivity; and Automotive solutions featuring AEC-Q100 qualified products for EV powertrain and ADAS applications. Each solution combines ST's semiconductor expertise with reference designs and software support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our solutions section to find the right ST-based solution for your application requirements.

faqs.question: How can ST solutions help accelerate my product development?
faqs.answer: ST solutions accelerate development through multiple mechanisms: Complete reference designs with schematics, PCB layouts, and bill of materials; Production-ready software including STM32Cube middleware and application examples; Evaluation hardware for rapid prototyping; Comprehensive documentation including application notes and user manuals; FAE support for design optimization and troubleshooting; Pre-certified solutions reducing regulatory compliance time. By leveraging these resources, customers can reduce time-to-market by 30-50% compared to ground-up development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team to discuss how ST solutions can accelerate your specific project timeline.

faqs.question: What support does LiTong provide for ST solution implementation?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive support for ST solution implementation including: Technical consultation for solution selection and customization; Hardware design review and optimization assistance; Software integration support including STM32Cube configuration; Supply chain management for solution components; Programming and testing services; Production scaling support. Our FAE team has deep expertise in ST's solution portfolio and can help adapt reference designs to your specific requirements while maintaining performance and reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical team to discuss your project requirements and receive personalized solution recommendations.

faqs.question: Are ST solutions suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, ST offers numerous automotive-qualified solutions featuring AEC-Q100 components and ISO 26262 functional safety support. Automotive solutions include: EV Traction Inverter solutions with SiC MOSFETs and gate drivers; Battery Management System solutions with isolated communication and monitoring; ADAS solutions with image sensors and high-performance processors; Body Electronics solutions with automotive-grade microcontrollers. All automotive solutions undergo rigorous qualification testing and include safety documentation for functional safety applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our automotive FAE team for AEC-Q100 qualified solutions and functional safety documentation.

faqs.question: How do I customize ST reference designs for my application?
faqs.answer: Customizing ST reference designs involves several steps: Review the reference design documentation to understand the architecture and component selection; Identify modifications needed for your specific requirements (voltage levels, current ratings, interface requirements); Use ST's simulation tools to verify modifications; Implement changes following ST's layout guidelines; Test and validate the modified design; Consider thermal and EMI implications of changes. LiTong's FAE team can assist with customization while ensuring design reliability and performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with the closest reference design and work with our FAE team to customize for your requirements.

solutions.name: High-Efficiency Motor Control Solution
solutions.description: Complete motor control solution featuring STM32 microcontrollers, STPOWER IGBTs/MOSFETs, and isolated gate drivers for industrial and automotive motor drive applications with up to 98% efficiency.
features: STPOWER IGBTs and MOSFETs up to 1200V

coreAdvantages.title: High Efficiency
coreAdvantages.description: Advanced FOC algorithm with optimized PWM patterns achieves up to 98% efficiency, reducing energy consumption and cooling requirements. ST's low-Rds(on) MOSFETs and fast-switching IGBTs minimize conduction and switching losses.

coreAdvantages.title: Robust Protection
coreAdvantages.description: Comprehensive protection including overcurrent, overvoltage, undervoltage, overtemperature, short-circuit, and ground fault protection. Isolated gate drivers with DESAT protection ensure safe operation under fault conditions.

coreAdvantages.title: Fast Time to Market
coreAdvantages.description: Complete reference design with production-ready software reduces development time by 6-12 months. STM32 ecosystem provides extensive libraries and tools for customization.

coreAdvantages.title: Scalable Architecture
coreAdvantages.description: Modular design supports power range from 0.5kW to 250kW with common control platform. Easy to scale from low-power industrial drives to high-power EV traction applications.

customerCases.challenge: A leading industrial automation company needed a high-performance servo drive solution with precise position control, fast torque response, and high efficiency for their next-generation CNC machines. The existing solution used discrete components with inconsistent performance and high energy consumption.
customerCases.solution: Implemented ST's motor control solution featuring STM32F407 MCU with FOC algorithm, STW48N60M2 MOSFETs for the inverter stage, and STGAP2SICS isolated gate drivers. The solution included sensorless vector control with encoder feedback option for high-precision applications.
customerCases.results: Achieved 97.5% efficiency at rated load, reducing energy consumption by 15% compared to previous design. Position accuracy improved to ±0.01% with <2ms torque response. The compact design reduced cabinet size by 30%. Product passed CE certification and entered production within 8 months.

customerCases.challenge: An EV startup required a compact, high-efficiency traction inverter for their electric delivery van. Key requirements included 98%+ efficiency, 100kW peak power, functional safety compliance (ISO 26262), and compact size for vehicle integration.
customerCases.solution: Designed custom traction inverter using ST's SiC MOSFETs (SCTW90N65G2V) for the power stage, STM32H7 high-performance MCU for control, and comprehensive safety architecture. Leveraged ST's automotive-qualified components and reference designs.
customerCases.results: Achieved 98.2% peak efficiency with SiC devices, exceeding target. Power density reached 25kW/L, enabling compact vehicle integration. Solution achieved ASIL-D functional safety rating. The inverter passed automotive qualification testing and is now in production for 10,000+ vehicles annually.

faeInsights.insight: Motor control is one of the most demanding applications for power electronics, requiring precise timing, robust protection, and high efficiency. Through my 18 years of experience supporting motor drive designs, I've found that the key to success lies in three areas: proper gate drive design, current sensing accuracy, and thermal management. ST's integrated solution addresses all three with isolated gate drivers providing reliable switching, high-precision current sensing enabling accurate FOC control, and comprehensive thermal design guidelines. The STM32 ecosystem is particularly valuable - the Motor Control Workbench tool can auto-generate FOC code, reducing software development from months to weeks. For high-power applications, I always recommend SiC MOSFETs over IGBTs when switching frequency exceeds 10kHz, as the efficiency gains justify the cost premium. The modular architecture of this solution allows customers to start with a lower power rating and scale up using the same control platform, protecting their software investment.
faeInsights.logic: The motor control solution follows a systematic design approach: First, select the appropriate power devices based on voltage/current requirements and switching frequency - use SiC for high-frequency applications above 650V, super-junction MOSFETs for 400-600V, and IGBTs for lower frequency high-current applications. Second, implement proper gate drive with adequate drive strength and protection features - isolated drivers are essential for safety and noise immunity. Third, design current sensing with sufficient bandwidth and accuracy for the control algorithm - shunt resistors offer best cost-performance, while isolated amplifiers provide highest safety ratings. Fourth, implement comprehensive protection including overcurrent, overvoltage, and thermal protection with appropriate fault response. Finally, optimize the thermal design to maintain junction temperatures within safe limits under worst-case conditions. The STM32 provides the processing power for advanced control algorithms while the rich peripheral set handles communication and I/O.
keyTakeaways: SiC MOSFETs enable 98%+ efficiency and should be used for high-frequency applications above 650V
Isolated gate drivers with DESAT protection are essential for reliable operation
STM32 Motor Control Workbench can auto-generate FOC code, reducing development time significantly
Proper thermal design is critical - calculate worst-case losses and verify junction temperatures
Modular architecture allows scaling from 0.5kW to 250kW with common control platform
commonPitfalls: Insufficient gate drive strength leading to slow switching and high losses
Inadequate current sensing bandwidth causing control instability
Poor PCB layout with high parasitic inductance causing voltage spikes
Insufficient thermal design leading to overtemperature shutdowns
Inadequate protection features resulting in catastrophic failures
bestPractices: Use 4-layer PCB with dedicated power and ground planes
Implement Kelvin connections for current sensing
Place decoupling capacitors close to power device terminals
Use isolated gate drivers with Miller clamp for SiC devices
Implement comprehensive protection with appropriate fault response
Follow ST's layout guidelines for minimized parasitic inductance

faqs.question: What motor types are supported by this solution?
faqs.answer: The motor control solution supports various motor types including AC induction motors (IM), permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSM), brushless DC motors (BLDC), and synchronous reluctance motors (SynRM). The FOC algorithm can be configured for different motor parameters including pole pairs, inductance, and back-EMF characteristics. Sensorless control is supported for most motor types using high-frequency injection or back-EMF estimation. For highest performance applications, encoder or resolver feedback can be added.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for motor parameter configuration and control algorithm optimization for your specific motor.

faqs.question: How do I implement sensorless control for my motor?
faqs.answer: Sensorless control uses the STM32 to estimate rotor position from motor currents and voltages. The solution includes advanced observer algorithms that work across the speed range: High-frequency injection for zero and low-speed operation where back-EMF is insufficient; Back-EMF estimation for medium to high-speed operation; Smooth transition between methods. Key parameters include observer gains, injection frequency, and transition speed. The STM32 Motor Control Workbench provides tools to tune these parameters for your specific motor.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use STM32 Motor Control Workbench to configure sensorless parameters. Start with auto-tuning and refine manually.

faqs.question: What safety features are included in the solution?
faqs.answer: The motor control solution includes comprehensive safety features: Hardware protection including overcurrent detection with <1us response, DESAT protection for IGBTs, undervoltage lockout, and overtemperature shutdown; Software protection including overcurrent, overvoltage, undervoltage, overload, stall detection, and ground fault detection; Safe torque off (STO) function compliant with IEC 61800-5-2; Isolated gate drivers provide reinforced isolation between control and power; Diagnostic features include fault logging and predictive maintenance indicators.
faqs.decisionGuide: All protection features are configurable. Contact us for safety certification support and SIL/PL rating documentation.

faqs.question: How do I scale the solution for different power ratings?
faqs.answer: The solution uses a modular architecture allowing easy scaling: Control board with STM32 remains common across power ratings; Power stage scales by changing the number and rating of power devices; Gate drivers scale with power device requirements; Current sensing scales with shunt resistor value and amplifier gain; Cooling system scales with power dissipation requirements. The same software can be used across the power range with only parameter changes for current limits and protection thresholds.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with reference design closest to your target power and modify power stage components. Contact us for scaling guidelines.

faqs.question: What communication interfaces are supported?
faqs.answer: The motor control solution supports multiple industrial communication protocols: Standard interfaces include RS485 (Modbus RTU), CAN (CANopen, DeviceNet), and EtherCAT for real-time control; The STM32 Ethernet MAC supports industrial Ethernet protocols; USB interface for configuration and diagnostics; Optional wireless connectivity for IoT applications. Protocol stacks are available through STM32Cube middleware. Custom protocols can be implemented using the available UART, SPI, and I2C interfaces.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select communication interface based on your system architecture. EtherCAT recommended for multi-axis synchronized systems.

faqs.question: What is the typical development timeline for a custom motor drive?
faqs.answer: Using ST's motor control solution significantly reduces development time: Hardware design and prototyping typically takes 4-6 weeks using reference design as starting point; Software development takes 6-8 weeks using STM32 Motor Control Workbench for FOC code generation; Testing and validation takes 4-6 weeks including thermal testing and protection verification; Certification (if required) takes 4-8 weeks depending on standards. Total time from concept to production is typically 4-6 months compared to 12-18 months for ground-up development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed project planning and timeline estimation for your specific requirements.

solutions.name: IoT Sensor Node Solution
solutions.description: Ultra-low-power IoT sensor node solution combining STM32L4 microcontrollers, MEMS sensors, and sub-GHz wireless connectivity for battery-operated industrial monitoring and smart building applications with 5+ year battery life.
features: 5+ year battery life on coin cell

coreAdvantages.title: Ultra-Low Power
coreAdvantages.description: Advanced power management with STM32L4's multiple low-power modes achieves <10uA average consumption. Smart duty cycling and sensor hub functionality enable 5+ year battery life on a single coin cell.

coreAdvantages.title: Comprehensive Sensing
coreAdvantages.description: Multi-sensor integration provides motion, environmental, and atmospheric data in a single compact node. ST's MEMS sensors offer industry-leading accuracy and stability.

coreAdvantages.title: Long-Range Connectivity
coreAdvantages.description: Sub-GHz LoRa connectivity enables transmission ranges up to 15km in open areas or through multiple building floors, eliminating the need for mesh networking infrastructure.

coreAdvantages.title: Rapid Deployment
coreAdvantages.description: Complete reference design with pre-certified wireless modules and cloud connectivity examples enables rapid prototyping and deployment. STM32 ecosystem provides extensive software support.

customerCases.challenge: A smart building company needed wireless sensors to monitor temperature, humidity, and occupancy across a 50,000 sqm office complex. Requirements included 5+ year battery life, long-range connectivity through concrete floors, and seamless integration with their building management system.
customerCases.solution: Deployed ST-based IoT sensor nodes featuring STM32L4 MCU, HTS221 for environmental sensing, LSM6DS3 for occupancy detection via motion sensing, and LoRa connectivity. Custom firmware implemented adaptive reporting based on change detection.
customerCases.results: Achieved 6+ year battery life with 15-minute reporting intervals. LoRa range exceeded 500m through multiple concrete floors, eliminating need for repeaters. System monitors 2,000+ zones with 99.5% uptime. Energy savings of 25% achieved through optimized HVAC control based on occupancy data.

customerCases.challenge: A manufacturer of industrial pumps needed a wireless vibration monitoring solution for predictive maintenance. Requirements included continuous vibration monitoring, 5-year battery life in harsh industrial environments, and reliable data transmission across a 2km factory floor.
customerCases.solution: Implemented custom sensor node using ST's LSM6DS3 IMU for vibration analysis, STM32L4 for edge processing with FFT analysis, and LoRa for data transmission. Machine learning algorithms running on the STM32 detect bearing faults and misalignment.
customerCases.results: Successfully detected 95% of developing faults 2-4 weeks before failure, reducing unplanned downtime by 80%. Battery life exceeded 5 years with continuous monitoring. System paid for itself within 6 months through prevented failures. Now deployed across 5,000+ pumps in multiple facilities.

faeInsights.insight: IoT sensor nodes represent one of the most challenging design problems in electronics - achieving multi-year battery life while providing useful data and reliable connectivity. Through my experience supporting dozens of IoT deployments, I've learned that success requires optimization at every level. The STM32L4 is exceptional for this application, with its sub-uA standby currents and fast wake-up times. But the real key is in the system architecture - using the sensor hub capability to batch data, implementing smart duty cycling, and leveraging edge processing to reduce transmission overhead. ST's MEMS sensors are ideal for IoT with their built-in FIFOs and autonomous features that can wake the MCU only when significant events occur. For wireless, LoRa is the clear choice for industrial applications requiring long range and penetration through obstacles. The trade-off is lower data rates, but for typical sensor applications reporting every few minutes, it's perfect. One often overlooked aspect is the power supply design - using a high-efficiency LDO rather than a switching regulator can actually improve battery life in these ultra-low-power applications due to the quiescent current savings.
faeInsights.logic: The IoT sensor node design follows a power-first methodology: First, establish the power budget by calculating energy consumption for each operating mode - sleep, sensor reading, processing, and transmission. The goal is to maximize time in sleep mode while meeting application requirements. Second, select components based on both active and sleep currents - the STM32L4 excels here with 130nA shutdown mode and fast wake-up. Third, implement aggressive duty cycling where the node sleeps between readings, using the RTC for periodic wake-up or sensor interrupts for event-driven wake-up. Fourth, use the sensor FIFO to batch multiple readings, allowing the MCU to stay asleep longer and reducing the overhead of wake-up/sleep transitions. Fifth, implement edge processing to reduce data transmission - for example, only transmit when sensor values change significantly rather than on a fixed schedule. Sixth, optimize the wireless protocol - use confirmed transmission only for critical data, leverage adaptive data rates, and implement efficient encoding. The result is an average current in the single-digit microamps, enabling multi-year battery life.
keyTakeaways: STM32L4's sub-uA sleep modes and fast wake-up are essential for long battery life
Use sensor FIFOs and hub functionality to batch data and reduce MCU wake frequency
Implement edge processing to reduce wireless transmission - this saves more power than local processing consumes
LoRa provides excellent range and penetration for industrial IoT applications
Consider total energy consumption including quiescent currents when selecting power supply components
commonPitfalls: Using switching regulators where LDOs would be more efficient at these power levels
Waking MCU for every sensor reading instead of using FIFO batching
Transmitting raw data instead of processed information, wasting wireless power
Inadequate antenna design reducing range and requiring higher transmit power
Poor sleep mode implementation with peripherals left powered unnecessarily
bestPractices: Implement multi-layered sleep with progressively deeper states
Use sensor autonomous features (threshold detection, FIFO) to minimize MCU wake-ups
Implement adaptive reporting - frequent when active, infrequent when stable
Optimize antenna matching for maximum efficiency
Use energy harvesting where available to extend battery life indefinitely
Implement over-the-air firmware updates for long-term maintainability

faqs.question: How is the 5+ year battery life achieved?
faqs.answer: The extended battery life is achieved through multiple techniques: STM32L4 ultra-low-power modes with 130nA shutdown current; Aggressive duty cycling with typical 0.1% active duty cycle; Sensor FIFO batching reducing MCU wake-ups by 10x; Edge processing reducing wireless transmissions; Optimized LoRa parameters with adaptive data rate; High-efficiency LDO with low quiescent current. The average power consumption is under 10uA, which with a 225mAh CR2032 battery provides 6+ years of operation. Actual battery life depends on reporting frequency and sensor activity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Battery life can be extended further by reducing reporting frequency or implementing local data logging with periodic bulk upload.

faqs.question: What wireless protocols are supported?
faqs.answer: The solution supports multiple wireless protocols: LoRaWAN for long-range low-power wide-area networks with range up to 15km; Sigfox as an alternative LPWAN technology; Sub-GHz proprietary protocols for custom applications; BLE 5.0 for short-range smartphone connectivity (optional add-on). The SX1262 transceiver is software-configurable for different protocols and frequency bands (868MHz EU, 915MHz US, 433MHz Asia). Gateway connectivity is available through commercial LoRaWAN networks or private gateways.
faqs.decisionGuide: LoRaWAN recommended for most applications due to ecosystem maturity and coverage. Contact us for network planning assistance.

faqs.question: How do I configure the sensor thresholds and reporting intervals?
faqs.answer: Sensor configuration is flexible and can be done through multiple methods: Over-the-air (OTA) configuration via downlink messages for deployed devices; Local configuration via UART or BLE during installation; Pre-configuration during manufacturing. Configurable parameters include: Reporting interval (1 minute to 24 hours), Sensor thresholds for event-driven reporting, Motion detection sensitivity, Alarm conditions and escalation, Data aggregation methods. The STM32 firmware implements a configuration manager that stores settings in non-volatile memory.
faqs.decisionGuide: Default configuration optimized for typical building automation. Contact us for custom firmware with specialized configuration requirements.

faqs.question: What cloud platforms are compatible with this solution?
faqs.answer: The IoT sensor node is compatible with major cloud platforms: AWS IoT Core via LoRaWAN network server integration; Microsoft Azure IoT Hub with direct integration; Google Cloud IoT Core support; IBM Watson IoT Platform; Generic MQTT brokers for custom implementations; Private cloud deployments via standard LoRaWAN packet forwarder. Reference implementations and code examples are available for AWS and Azure. The solution uses standard JSON data format for easy integration with any platform.
faqs.decisionGuide: AWS IoT and Azure IoT Hub have the most mature integrations. Contact us for platform-specific implementation support.

faqs.question: Is the solution suitable for outdoor applications?
faqs.answer: The base solution is designed for indoor use with IP20 rating. For outdoor applications, an IP67-rated enclosure is recommended. The ST components are rated for -40°C to +85°C industrial temperature range, suitable for most outdoor environments. Additional considerations for outdoor deployment include: UV-resistant enclosure material, Condensation protection with conformal coating or desiccant, Lightning protection for outdoor antenna installations, Higher transmit power may be needed for long-range outdoor links. Extended temperature versions (-40°C to +105°C) available for extreme environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for outdoor deployment guidelines and IP67 enclosure recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I implement over-the-air firmware updates?
faqs.answer: Over-the-air (OTA) firmware updates are implemented using the STM32L4's dual-bank Flash memory: New firmware is received via LoRa downlink and stored in the inactive Flash bank; CRC verification ensures firmware integrity; On next reboot, the bootloader switches to the new firmware; Rollback capability if new firmware fails to start. The update process is optimized for low bandwidth - differential updates reduce data size by 80-90%. Typical update time is 10-30 minutes depending on file size and network conditions. FUOTA (Firmware Update Over The Air) is supported through LoRaWAN standard.
faqs.decisionGuide: OTA updates recommended for deployments >100 devices. Contact us for FUOTA implementation guidelines.
